summary:
Downward and inward forces are commonly utilized in treating overbites but may also increase open-bite conditions depending on application techniques; thus, their use requires careful management considering individual anatomy variations. The primary effect tends toward correcting overbites rather than universally increasing them as suggested strongly enough alone without acknowledging variable outcomes due to differing factors influencing treatment success rates significantly across patients. To summarize Agent2’s reasoning into two concise sentences focusing only on critical aspects related directly back to your query about what such directional forces could potentially achieve clinically speaking...
